notes: All evals assume the subagent runs with cwd set to a real codebase checkout. Default target is this skills repo (my-claude-code) so ideation has concrete code to ground against. To benchmark against a different codebase, set the subagent's working directory accordingly.

evals.name: what-next-casual
evals.prompt: been grinding on this for months and kinda running out of ideas, what would you build next if this were yours?
evals.expected_output: A markdown report at ./ideas/reports/<date>-ideate.md containing: codebase characterisation, interview summary, market landscape, premises confirmed with user, desired outcome, 5-8 tiered ideas grouped under opportunities, and a recommended next step with concrete build steps.

assertions.text: A report file is created in ./ideas/reports/ with filename matching YYYY-MM-DD-ideate.md (or -N suffix)
Report contains a Codebase Characterisation section with answers to 'What is this?', 'Who is it for?', and an implicit thesis
Report contains a Premises section with at least 2 numbered premise statements
Report contains a Market Landscape section naming at least 2 distinct competitors or adjacent tools
Report contains a Desired Outcome section
Report contains at least 5 distinct idea entries under Opportunities, each with a title and tier label
Ideas span at least 2 of the 3 tiers (adjacent / expansion / moonshot)
Every idea has a non-empty 'Why this codebase is uniquely positioned' (or equivalent) field that references specific observed code, modules, or architecture — not generic aspiration
Report ends with a Recommended Next Step section containing at least 2 concrete build steps (not validation tasks like 'interview users')
The agent invoked AskUserQuestion at least once during the run (for interview and/or premise confirmation)
The agent invoked WebSearch at least once during the run (for market scan)

evals.name: competitive-gap-hunt
evals.prompt: i want to understand where this sits in the market and where it could actually differentiate vs what's already out there — feels like a lot of tools in this space and i don't want to build something that already exists
evals.expected_output: A markdown report emphasising competitive analysis: market landscape with 2-3 direct competitors and explicit gap identification, Eureka moments where first-principles thinking contradicts conventional wisdom, and ideas whose 'uniquely positioned' field is grounded in competitive gaps.

assertions.text: A report file is created in ./ideas/reports/ with filename matching YYYY-MM-DD-ideate.md (or -N suffix)
Report contains a Market Landscape section naming at least 3 distinct tools (direct competitors and/or adjacent tools)
Each competitor entry in the Market Landscape includes an explicit weakness or gap — not just a feature summary
Report contains at least 5 distinct idea entries
At least 2 ideas' 'uniquely positioned' justifications explicitly reference a gap or weakness identified in the market scan
Report contains a Premises section with at least 2 numbered premise statements
Ideas span at least 2 of the 3 tiers (adjacent / expansion / moonshot)
Report ends with concrete build steps, not business validation tasks
The agent invoked WebSearch at least twice (non-trivial market scan)

evals.name: personal-to-product
evals.prompt: this started as a personal project but i'm wondering if there's a version of it other people would actually want to use. where would i take it to make that real?
evals.expected_output: A report with strong premise challenge around audience expansion, distribution considered as part of at least one idea's feasibility, and the Desired Outcome framed around broader adoption.

assertions.text: A report file is created in ./ideas/reports/ with filename matching YYYY-MM-DD-ideate.md (or -N suffix)
Report contains a Premises section where at least one premise concerns target audience, adoption, or distribution
At least one idea's 'Architectural leverage' or 'Risks' field explicitly discusses distribution (how users would get the thing — package manager, releases, install flow, etc.)
Desired Outcome section is framed around broader use or adoption, not purely internal improvement
Report contains at least 5 distinct idea entries
Ideas span at least 2 of the 3 tiers (adjacent / expansion / moonshot)
Every idea has a non-empty 'uniquely positioned' field grounded in observed code, not generic aspiration
Report ends with concrete build steps for the recommended next idea
The agent invoked AskUserQuestion at least twice (interview + premise confirmation)

evals.name: context-file-respected
evals.expected_output: When ./ideas/CONTEXT.md exists, the skill reads it in Phase 0, surfaces the loaded context to the user for confirmation, skips redundant characterisation and competitive re-derivation, dedupes ideas against the implemented/proposed lists, and updates the file at the end.

files.content:
---
last_updated: 2025-11-01
---

# TestRepo — Ideation Context

## Positioning

**What is this?** A CLI tool for generating structured project docs from code.

**Who is it for?** Solo developers and small teams.

**Implicit thesis** Developers write better docs when the tool meets them in the terminal.

## Problems it solves

- Doc drift between code and docs
- Onboarding friction

## Competitive landscape

**Docusaurus**
Strong at: polished static sites
Weak at: keeping docs in sync with code automatically

## First-principles insights

- 2025-10-15 **Docs-as-tests** — the market assumes docs are a writing problem; this codebase treats it as a diffing problem

## Implemented ideas

- 2025-09-01 Auto-codemap generation — generates architecture codemaps from code analysis

## Proposed ideas (pending)

- 2025-11-01 Diff-based doc freshness score — surface stale sections as a CLI warning

assertions.text: The agent reads ./ideas/CONTEXT.md in Phase 0 (before Phase 1 characterisation)
The agent surfaces the loaded context to the user in Phase 0 — e.g. mentions last_updated date and current positioning
The agent does NOT re-propose 'Auto-codemap generation' (already in Implemented ideas) or 'Diff-based doc freshness score' (already in Proposed ideas)
A report file is created in ./ideas/reports/ with filename matching YYYY-MM-DD-ideate.md (or -N suffix)
The agent updates ./ideas/CONTEXT.md at the end of the run (Phase 7) — bumps last_updated and appends the new recommended idea to Proposed ideas (pending)
The agent asks the user if any previously proposed ideas shipped (Phase 7 question) before updating the context file

evals.name: context-file-bootstrap-offer
evals.expected_output: When ./ideas/CONTEXT.md does not exist, the skill runs normal ideation and at the end offers to bootstrap the context file from Phase 1 characterisation, Phase 3 competitive findings, and a best-guess implemented-ideas list from merged PRs/git history.

assertions.text: A report file is created in ./ideas/reports/ with filename matching YYYY-MM-DD-ideate.md (or -N suffix)
After writing the report, the agent asks the user whether they want to save a canonical ideation context (./ideas/CONTEXT.md)
If the user agrees to bootstrap, the agent runs git log --merges or gh pr list to infer previously shipped ideas
If the user agrees to bootstrap, the resulting ./ideas/CONTEXT.md contains Positioning, Competitive landscape, and Proposed ideas (pending) sections populated from this run
The agent does NOT create ./ideas/CONTEXT.md without asking the user first
